prediction.summary: A distributed credential stuffing attack targeted the 'web' server, attempting to compromise the 'user@corp' account across both SSH and web login services from multiple source IP addresses. After numerous failed attempts, an attacker successfully authenticated to both the web application and SSH using the 'user@corp' credentials from IP 198.51.100.123. An internal audit system detected distributed credential reuse, indicating a successful account compromise.

Failed SSH password attempt for user@corp from 198.51.100.101
Failed web login attempt (HTTP 401) for /login from 198.51.100.112
Successful SSH password authentication for user@corp from 198.51.100.123
Distributed credential reuse detected across 8 source IPs for account user@corp

recommendations: Immediately disable or reset the password for 'user@corp' and enforce Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) if not already in place.
Isolate the 'web' server for forensic analysis to determine the extent of compromise and identify any post-exploitation activities.
Investigate all source IPs involved (198.51.100.12, .33, .44, .55, .66, .77, .88, .99, .101, .112, .123) for other malicious activity across the network.
Review all logs associated with 'user@corp' for any unauthorized actions, data exfiltration, or privilege escalation attempts after the successful login.
Implement or strengthen IP-based rate limiting and account lockout policies on all public-facing login services (SSH, web applications) to mitigate future brute-force or credential stuffing attacks.
